methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $70M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
ISEE, a Series B-stage drone and autonomous systems company founded by Yibiao Zhao, has raised $70M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparison universe of eight private peers in the Drones & Autonomous sector, including Elroy Air, Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, and Archer Aviation. No public comparables exist in this nascent category.

A stage-progression methodology was applied, using the $70M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B progression. This yielded an implied valuation of $133M. A peer-median approach was not viable; while five same-sector peers exist, filtering for same-stage or adjacent-stage companies produced fewer than three datapoints, making a narrow comparable analysis unreliable. A broad sector median would introduce excessive noise and was therefore excluded.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $113M to $153M, with a median point of $133M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the absence of tight-peer valuation data, limited public market benchmarks for the sector, and the reliance on raised capital as a proxy rather than on operational metrics. Users should weight this estimate accordingly and update as ISEE approaches Series C fundraising, when adjacent-stage comparables will become available.
